_comment: Valorant kill detection spec (EXPERIMENTAL). Detects the player's own kills by killfeed row structure (mode feedRow) rather than by matching a fixed banner color: the player's kill row reads [gold-boxed self-highlight][teal bar][red bar] left-to-right, the death row is the mirror image, and teammate/enemy rows carry no gold highlight at all. A kill fires when a feed band's gold/teal/red x-centroids fall in that order with enough separation - a signal that holds regardless of the player's weapon skin or game mode, unlike matching the killfeed banner's own color. minConsecutiveHits=1 because the gold highlight is brightest in a row's first ~0.5s; band height, per-class pixel mass, and centroid separation carry the false-positive rejection instead of requiring persistence. Hue windows assume Valorant's default (non-colorblind) palette; alternate palettes need their own spec. Tune with Settings > Kill Detection > Diagnostic mode.
